senses.gloss: to drip, to dribble, to trickle

examples.text: الْحَنَفِيَّةُ فِي الْمَطْبَخِ تَقْطُرُ، مِمَّا يَهْدِرُ الْمَاءُ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ.
examples.english: The faucet in the kitchen drips, wasting water every day.

senses.gloss: to let drip, to let dribble, to infuse in driblets

examples.english: He dripped the medicine into his eyes.

senses.gloss: to let drip, to let dribble, to infuse in driblets

examples.english: He dripped the medicine into his eyes.

senses.gloss: to filter, to refine, to distill
to form a train of camels, to line up camels in single file (connected with halters)
to couple, to tow, to tug
region, quarter, district, section, zone, tract, country, land
Qatar (a country in West Asia in the Middle East)
agalloch, agarwood, aloeswood, eaglewood; Aquilaria malaccensis (syn. Aquilaria agallocha)

examples.text: آتُونِي زُبَرَ الْحَدِيدِ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا سَاوَىٰ بَيْنَ الصَّدَفَيْنِ قَالَ ٱنْفُخُوا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَعَلَهُ نَارًا قَالَ آتُونِي أُفْرِغْ عَلَيْهِ قِطْرًا
examples.english: “Give me bits of iron.” Until he dumped equally high between the two mountain cuttings. He said: “Blow.” Till he made fire. He said: “Bring me molten copper I pour upon it.“
We made the spring of molten copper flow for him.
